titleStmt.title: Copper plates from Kondavidu (set III) â€” reign of Prج¥thivؤ«إ›rؤ«mإ«la, year 43

editionStmt.p: First digital edition made by أ‰cole franأ§aise d'Extrأھme-Orient (Paris, France), realized in collaboration with the HiSoMA Research Centre (Lyon, France) and hosted by TGIR Huma-Num (France) as Early Inscriptions of ؤ€ndhradeإ›a, in 2015-2017.

encodingDesc.p: Encoded in TEI/EpiDoc according to the DHARMA Encoding Guide for Documentary Editions, version 2

revisionDesc.change: EIAD file transformed to follow the DHARMA encoding structure. Metadata extracted to be checked and updated according DHARMA workflow. Done through XSLT.

div.ab:
svasti *إ›rؤ«vijayanandivarddhanavؤپsakؤپd devadvijagurucaraل¹‡ؤپravinda
samؤپrؤپdhanؤپdhigatavipulaniratiإ›ayapuل¹‡yanicayasya sakala
diل¹…maل¹‡ل¸چalؤپlaل¹پkؤپrabhإ«tayaإ›asaل¸¥ إ›rؤ«prabhؤپkaravikhyؤپtamahؤپrؤپja
sya sإ«nuل¸¥ mؤپtؤپpitrج¥pؤپdؤپnuddhyؤپtaل¸¥ إ›rutismrج¥tivihitapadؤپrtthؤپvabodha
janitaprajأ±ؤپvivekaniravagؤ«tؤپnupؤپlitؤپإ›eل¹£avarل¹‡ل¹‡ؤپإ›ramadha
rmmaل¸¥ prajؤپparirakل¹£aل¹‡opajanitapuل¹‡yopacayaprayojanؤپvؤپptarؤپ
jyagurubhؤپraل¸¥ pإ«rvvarؤپjarل¹£imaryyؤپdؤپnatikramaل¹‡ؤ«yadharmmaل¸¥ Asakrج¥
danekaniإ›itanistriل¹پإ›asahasrasaل¹پkulؤپtibhؤ«masaل¹پgrؤپmabhإ«miprati
2labdhavijayؤ« إ›aإ›ؤ«va prajؤپnandakaraل¸¥ udadhir iva gabhؤ«rasvatvaل¸¥
Aharahas trisandhyؤپm iل¹£ل¹­adevatؤپrؤپdhanena gobhإ«mihiraل¹‡yakanyؤپ
pradؤپnؤپdibhiإ› ca saphalؤ«krج¥takؤپyacittopakaraل¹‡aل¸¥ paramamؤپ
heإ›varaل¸¥ paramabrahmaل¹‡yaل¸¥ إ›rؤ«manآ· prithiviإ›rؤ«mإ«larؤپjaل¸¥ svaviل¹£ayani
vؤپsinaل¸¥ sarvvؤپn evam ؤپjأ±ؤپpayati yathؤپ viditam astu bhavatؤپmآ· bhuvanavi
dite إ›rؤ«guل¹‡apؤپإ›apure إ›ailendrapإ«rvvadigbhؤپganitambe matpratiل¹£ل¹­hؤپpita
إ›rؤ«lokavikhyؤپtamahؤپvihؤپranivؤپsinaل¸¥ Aل¹£ل¹­ؤپdaإ›anikؤپyacؤپtu
rddiإ›ؤپryyavarabhikل¹£usaل¹پghasya cؤ«varapiل¹‡ل¸چapؤپtaإ›aiyyؤپsanaglؤپnapra
3pratyayabhaiل¹£ajyapariل¹£karopabhogؤپya tؤپل¸»upؤپkkaviل¹£aye karakuل¹‡ل¸چakaل¹پ
sؤپryyaveل¸·kaل¹‡ل¸چakurikimaل¹‡imarddipallaveأ±canؤپmadheyagrؤپmamaddhyagataل¸¥ Aparaإ›ailanivؤپsinan navakarmmika
m adhiل¹£ل¹­hؤپya Arل¹­ل¹­aluvuل¸·آ· nؤپma grؤپmas sarvvabؤپdhaparihؤپreل¹‡a mayؤپ dattaل¸¥ tatra na ke
na cit paribؤپdhؤپ karaل¹‡ؤ«yؤپ bhavatti cؤپtra manugؤ«tؤپ إ›lokؤپل¸¥

pravarddhamؤپnavijayarؤپjyasaل¹پvatsare
tracatvؤپriل¹پإ›ati varttamؤپne phؤپlguل¹‡amؤپsaإ›uklapakل¹£asya
trج¥tiyؤپyؤپmآ· varttamؤپnؤپyؤپل¹پ إ›ؤپsanam aل¹پkitamآ· .

app.note: The first word is engraved in the left margin.
There is no kra to correct to krج¥ . See a clear example of krج¥ in l. 10.
Krishna Sastry does not note the scribal deletion.
A kؤپkapؤپda is marked above the إ›a, but no correction or insertion seems to be made nor is any expected.
According to Krishna Sastry, this must be corrected to -bؤپdhؤپpari-. But the reading -bؤپdhؤپpari- is not attested anywhere in these inscriptions.
Krishna Sastry emends to bhatrج¥-. Neither Krishna Sastryâ€™ reading nor his emendation is correct.
Despite the reading, the interpretation of Krishna Sastry and his contemporaries must have been that this must be emended to tricatvؤپriل¹پإ›ati, and this is indeed what must be done.

div.p: Hail! From the victorious camp of Nandivardhana, the illustrious king Prج¥thivؤ«إ›rؤ«mإ«la, blessed by the feet of his parents; upholder of all laws on class and stage of life, in impeccable manner, through [his] intelligence and discrimination produced by [his] perception of the meanings of the words enjoined in revelation and tradition; who had attained the heavy burden of sovereignty with the purpose of accumulating merit produced by protecting his subjects; for whom the rules of the Old Royal Sage (i.e. Manu) were an unbreakable law; who had won victory more than once on battle grounds that were excessively terrifying, dense with thousands of swords sharpened many times; who, like the moon, brought pleasure to his subjects; who, like the ocean, was of profound nature; who had brought fruit to his bodily and mental activities by daily worship of his preferred deity at the three junctures and by grants of cows, lands, gold and maidens, etc.; devout worshiper of Maheإ›vara; extremely devout; son of the celebrated great king إڑrؤ« Prabhؤپkara, whose fame was the ornament of all quarters of space, whose unsurpassed store of merit had been obtained by the veneration of the feet of gods, brahmins and masters â€” he commands all residents of his (?) own district, as follows:

â€کBe it known to you that, having endorsed (adhiل¹£ل¹­hؤپya) the officer in charge of constructions and renovations residing on the Aparaإ›aila (to oversee the gift), the village named Arل¹­ل¹­aluvuل¸·, being in the middle of the villages called Karakuل¹‡ل¸چa, Kaل¹پsؤپryya, Veل¸·kaل¹‡ل¸چa, Kuriki, Maل¹‡imarddi and Pallaveأ±ca, in the Tؤپل¸»upؤپka district, has been given by meâ€”with complete exemption of taxesâ€”for the purpose of the requisites consisting in robe, alms-food, lodging, as well as medicine to cure the sick, of the community of noble excellent monks of the four quarters and eighteen schools, residing in the great monastery called إڑrؤ«-Loka, that had been established by myself on the eastern slope of the eminent mountain at the world-renowned إڑrؤ«-Guل¹‡apؤپإ›apura. No one may make any trouble in this connection. And there are verses pronounced by Manu on this matter:
The destiny of those who slay their master, a cow, a brahmin, a child, a woman or an ascetic, will be the one of him who seizes [the land/livelihood] that is marked by a (royal) charter.
Hear the fruit of the virtuous man who protects it scrupulously: during numerous thousands of aeons he plays in heaven with the gods.
There is not and will not be any higher gift than the gift of land. There is not and will not be any worse sin than the theft of the same.
The giver of land stays in heaven for sixty thousand years; the one who seizes [land] as well as the one who approves (of the seizing) will reside as many [years] in hell.
He who would seize land given by himself or by someone else becomes a worm in excrement and is cooked together with his ancestors.
By numerous [kings], land has been given; and by many it has been protected. Whoever holds land at a given moment, to him does the fruit then belong.
The charter has been dated in the forty-third year, current, of the victorious and prosperous reign, on the third [day], current, of the waxing fortnight of the month of Phؤپlguل¹‡a.
As long as moon, sun and oceans [last], and as long as this earth [lasts], so long may the command of إڑrؤ« Mإ«larؤپja protect this land!
First described and edited by : 74-5, 80-2. Re-edited here from the estampages published by Krishna Sastry and after autopsy of the plates.
